one_sentence: Most 'multi-agent' problems are single-agent + tools; add agents only when context isolation or parallel expertise demands it, then pick supervisor / swarm / sequential / hierarchical via two binary questions (do agents know each other? must output be one voice?).

rubric.Q0: Is single-agent + tools enough? YES -> single-agent, STOP.
rubric.Q1: Do agents need to know about each other? NO -> supervisor (or sequential if order fixed).
rubric.Q2: Must output speak with one voice / single audit funnel? YES -> supervisor; NO -> swarm.
rubric.scaling_override: >=6 specialists grouping into teams -> hierarchical (grouping only, NOT routing).

key_nuance: LangGraph's own benchmark: swarm slightly beats supervisor on accuracy AND uses fewer tokens, yet supervisor is the shipped default. The default encodes third-party-agent safety (single audit funnel), not accuracy. Do not copy the default blindly.

operation_candidates.name: DefendSingleAgentBaseline
operation_candidates.trigger: A task is being framed as 'a team of agents'.
action: List the proposed roles.
For each role, name the concrete failure that merging into one agent would cause (style drift / missed checklist / context bloat / required parallelism).
No nameable failure => single agent + tools.
operation_candidates.output: single-agent + tools, OR a justified list of must-split roles.

operation_candidates.name: SingleVsMultiGate
operation_candidates.trigger: Baseline defended; at least one role has a real split-justifying failure.
action: Confirm the force is context isolation or parallel expertise, not 'sounds more capable'.
If only the latter, stay single.
operation_candidates.output: Yes/no on multi-agent with the force named in one sentence.
evidence: crewai: §2.2 Phase 1.1 (one agent doing two jobs degrades)

operation_candidates.name: StaticOrderGate
operation_candidates.trigger: Multi-agent confirmed; order of work fixed at design time.
action: Choose SEQUENTIAL.
List agents in execution order.
Pass dependencies explicitly (CrewAI context=[...]); do not rely on implicit transfer.
operation_candidates.output: Ordered task list; CrewAI Process.sequential or LangGraph static edges.

operation_candidates.name: PeerAwarenessGate
operation_candidates.trigger: Routing must be decided at runtime.
action: Ask: do sub-agents know about each other?
NO => supervisor branch; YES => continue to OP-5.

operation_candidates.name: SingleVoiceGate
action: Ask: must output be one voice / single audit funnel?
YES => SUPERVISOR; NO => SWARM.

operation_candidates.name: ApplySupervisorDefaultCaveat
operation_candidates.trigger: Supervisor selected, OR about to accept a framework default.
action: Check trust: third-party/untrusted agents => supervisor is correct.
Internal/trusted => re-test whether swarm's lower token cost wins; if so, switch.
operation_candidates.output: Topology chosen on trust + the two questions, not on the default.
evidence: langgraph: Step 4 (swarm beats supervisor on bench, supervisor default for third-party safety)

operation_candidates.name: TuneBeforeSwitch
action: Apply published fixes to current topology first.
Supervisor: remove handoff messages, add forwarding-messages tool, optimise tool naming (~50% measured improvement).
Switch paradigm only if still over budget.
operation_candidates.output: Tuned topology or a justified migration.

operation_candidates.name: BoundTheTopology
action: allow_delegation=False on workers.
Per-agent max_iter; outer timeout.
Explicit state-based exit counter; trust nothing the LLM does to terminate.

dilemma_cases.title: Swarm beats supervisor on the benchmark, yet ship supervisor
dilemma_cases.tension: LangChain's own benchmark: swarm slightly outperforms supervisor and uses fewer tokens, but supervisor is the recommended default.
dilemma_cases.resolution: Decode the default's reason (third-party-agent safety + single audit funnel), not the default. Internal trusted agents with no single-voice mandate -> swarm. Single auditable funnel -> keep supervisor, apply the 3 fixes (~50%) before declaring it slow.
dilemma_cases.extracted_op: OP-6: a framework default encodes the author's worst-case constraint, not yours.

dilemma_cases.title: CrewAI hierarchical is structurally broken for simple routing
dilemma_cases.tension: Process.hierarchical looks like it auto-routes, but the manager executes ALL tasks and the last output overwrites; it does not skip on triage.
dilemma_cases.resolution: Hierarchical is coordination, not routing. ~5-line routing -> CrewAI Flow @router/@listen or LangGraph conditional edge. LLM-semantic routing -> custom manager_agent, never bare manager_llm. Bound it.
dilemma_cases.extracted_op: Hierarchical != router. Routing is control flow; make it explicit.

anti_patterns: Multi-agent theater (splitting with no context-isolation/parallel-expertise force).
Hierarchical / supervisor for simple if/else routing (runs everything).
Copying the supervisor default blindly (benchmark favours swarm for internal agents).
Agent-count explosion (>5 -> coordination failure; >=6 group into teams).
Picking topology on aesthetics instead of the two binary questions.
